notes: Canonical example data for ccanvil downstream node test fixtures. Reserved-namespace values (RFC 2606) are auto-allowlisted by .ccanvil/scripts/security-audit.sh's email scanner regex (lines matching @example\.(com|org|net) or @users\.noreply, plus the noreply@ prefix). Test fixtures across ccanvil downstream nodes SHOULD source fake user data from this file (or use exactly these values inline) so security-audit treats them as known-fake. Adding more entries is non-breaking — security-audit allowlists the entire @example.{com,org,net} namespace, not a specific list.

emails.context: system-sender fixture — use the address verbatim; safe because @example.com is in the RFC 2606 reserved namespace, not because of the noreply@ prefix alone
